what_it_does: Implements a grounded thematic determine operation over a pre-baked dragnet extraction asset: loads extraction records, filters candidates by semantic search or keyword regex, reranks with a served jina-v3 reranker, collects verbatim claims with provenance, asks a grouping model to cluster claims by index only, then reconstructs themes from the real claims and verifies no invented indices.
observations: The top-level determine() orchestrates candidate retrieval, reranking, claim collection, model grouping by claim index, and a no-fiction validity check.
Uses .data/store/extractions/extractions-<asset>.jsonl as its read-only source; supports assets 'full' and 'visual-dna'.
Caches loaded records and per-asset chunk indexes by file mtime/size to avoid repeated ~0.6s JSONL reloads.
Candidate filtering prefers semantic search over an 'extractions' embedding space, falling back to a keyword regex built from the topic.
Reranking calls a served jina-v3 endpoint at RERANK_URL with configurable timeout, truncation, and pool size.
Relevance distribution is surfaced via _relevance_read using a RELEVANCE_STRONG threshold and n_strong count.
collect_claims deduplicates substring claims within the same chunk to avoid fragment proliferation.
The grouping model is constrained to return only theme labels and claim index lists; determine() maps indices back to verbatim claims and flags invalid indices.
Many constants are env-configurable: RERANK_URL, RERANK_TIMEOUT, DETERMINE_RERANK_TRUNC, DETERMINE_RERANK_POOL, DETERMINE_GROUP_CTX, DETERMINE_RELEVANCE_STRONG.
Includes an opt-in honest-empty gate (min_strong) and an auto-trim guard when full claims exceed the chat model context budget.
standouts: Structural anti-confabulation: the LLM only clusters by claim index; every output claim is a verbatim stored extraction with chunk_id/rel_path/anchor provenance.
Semantic candidates plus jina-v3 reranker form a two-stage relevance gate, with score distribution exposed to callers.
Mtime/size keyed caches make repeated determine calls cheap while staying fresh after a rebake.
conventions: Extensive environment-variable knobs with documented defaults.
Detailed docstrings and inline comments explaining design rationale and provenance dates.
Reuse-don't-parallel: reranker endpoint shared with recollection recall; warm suite/store reused when available.
Pydantic BaseModel schemas for model outputs.
Declared notices instead of silent failures (rerank unavailable, stale asset, auto-trim, low relevance).
concerns: File handles opened without with-statement or explicit encoding in _load_recs and read_extraction.
semantic_candidates swallows all exceptions and returns None, hiding the real failure reason.
topic_regex ORs all salient keywords, which can match unrelated contexts and does not handle phrase boundaries.
Substring deduplication in collect_claims is O(n^2) within a chunk and sensitive only to whitespace-normalized substrings.
determine() is a long function with nested helper and multiple return points, mixing orchestration and formatting.
The address-classified guard for the numbered block is a brittle workaround.
notes: A defensive, grounded retrieval-to-themes module that makes the LLM organize existing extractions rather than invent claims, with env knobs and explicit freshness/relevance signals.
questions: What exact contract do cog.run_items and Role expect, and how does max_tokens map to the served chat-4b context?
How is the 'extractions' embedding space populated and what fields does suite.query_corpus return?
What callers set min_strong and how should they surface an honest-empty result to users?
Why does semantic_candidates cold-construct a Suite/NodeRegistry when no suite is passed instead of reusing a global one?
Is the RELEVANCE_STRONG threshold of 0.15 and n_strong>=3 'covered' rule calibrated per corpus or per deployment?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is exactly a single, reusable grounded determine implementation over dragnet extractions, used by both CLI and MCP, with no-fiction clustering.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make thematic summarization of a corpus trustworthy by ensuring every returned claim already exists in stored extractions, while giving callers visible signals about relevance, freshness, and confidence.
novelty: The index-only clustering pattern with post-hoc no-fiction verification and full provenance tracing (rel_path + anchor + chunk_id) is unusual compared to free-synthesis approaches.
contribution: Provides a reusable, observable, grounded thematic clustering path over pre-extracted claims, including semantic candidate retrieval, reranking, anti-confabulation verification, and freshness signaling, that a fused system could keep as the canonical determine implementation.
summary_for_embedding: A Python module that performs grounded thematic determination over a JSONL extraction asset by retrieving semantic or keyword candidates, reranking them, collecting verbatim claims, clustering by index with a small model, and returning themes with full source provenance and no-fiction validation.
intention_for_embedding: To give CLI and MCP callers a single, trustworthy, non-confabulating determine operation over the dragnet extraction layer.

symbols.does: Loads and mtime/size-caches extraction records from the asset JSONL.
symbols.description: Reads the extractions-<asset>.jsonl file and caches the parsed records keyed by file signature so repeated determine calls avoid reloading the whole file.

symbols.does: Returns the filesystem path for a named extraction asset.
symbols.description: Builds the path under .data/store/extractions using the provided asset name, defaulting to 'full'.

symbols.does: Concatenates searchable text fields of an extraction record.
symbols.description: Joins about, touches, summary, claims, and relations into a single string for keyword filtering and reranker input.

symbols.does: Reads a single extraction record by extraction:// URI.
symbols.description: Parses an extraction://<asset>/<chunk_id> address, builds a chunk_id index for the asset, and returns the full record; caches the index by file mtime/size.

symbols.does: Reports how old an extraction asset is and whether it exceeds a staleness threshold.
symbols.description: Returns bake epoch, age in days, record count, and an optional notice when the asset is older than stale_after_days; stays side-effect-free.

symbols.does: Builds a keyword regex from a natural-language topic.
symbols.description: Extracts non-stop words at least three characters long and ORs them for a cheap candidate filter over extraction blobs.

symbols.does: Retrieves meaning-nearest extraction records via the embedded 'extractions' space.
symbols.description: Queries the corpus embedding space for the topic, maps returned extraction:// ids back to records by chunk_id, and falls back to None on any error.

symbols.does: Re-scores candidates with the served jina-v3 reranker.
symbols.description: Sends truncated candidate blobs to the reranker endpoint, applies an optional score floor and top-n cap, and annotates survivors with _rerank_score; declares failures instead of silently skipping.

symbols.does: Surfaces the distribution of rerank scores as a relevance signal.
symbols.description: Counts per-chunk strong scores against RELEVANCE_STRONG and labels coverage as covered, thin, or likely-off-topic, with a caller-visible notice.

symbols.does: Collects verbatim claims from candidate records up to max_claims.
symbols.description: Takes pre-filtered candidates or keyword-filtered records, deduplicates substring claims within each chunk, and returns claim entries with full provenance and optional rerank score.

symbols.does: Pydantic schema for the grouping model output.
symbols.description: Requires only a list of themes containing a label and claim index numbers, preventing the model from writing claim text.

Loads records, selects semantic or keyword candidates, optionally reranks, collects claims, prompts a grouping model to cluster by index, maps indices back to real claims, verifies no-fiction, and returns themes with provenance, relevance, and freshness.

symbols.does: Formats numbered claim text for the grouping prompt.
symbols.description: Nested helper that builds the numbered claim list, optionally trimming each claim, and is used to fit the prompt within the chat model context budget.
